explanation: The requirement is about the specific behavior in PCV mode during the expiratory phase: if a spontaneous breathing effort is detected (i.e., a sudden drop in pressure above a threshold), a new breathing cycle must be triggered (which means starting the inspiration phase). The key conditions are: system is in PCV mode, currently in the expiratory phase, and a drop above the inhale trigger sensitivity (dropPAW_greaterThan_ITS_PCV) is detected. The property should capture that whenever these conditions (PCVMode & expiratoryPhase & dropPAW_greaterThan_ITS_PCV) hold, the system should immediately start the inspiratory phase (inspiratoryPhaseStart).
decision1_substring: Within the trigger window during the expiratory phase, in the case of spontaneous breathing, the ventilator shall trigger a new breathing cycle (i.e., it goes in inspiration phase) when it detects a sudden drop in pressure above the inhale trigger sensitivity (i.e., it yields the condition drop(PAW) > ITS_PCV ).
decision3_substring: the ventilator shall trigger a new breathing cycle (i.e., it goes in inspiration phase) when it detects a sudden drop in pressure above the inhale trigger sensitivity (i.e., it yields the condition drop(PAW) > ITS_PCV ).
output_structured_natural_language: whenever PCVMode & expiratoryPhase & dropPAW_greaterThan_ITS_PCV, immediately satisfy inspiratoryPhaseStart

explanation: This structured natural language translation captures the requirement that in PCV mode, during the expiratory phase and specifically within the trigger window, when a spontaneous breath is detected via a sudden drop in pressure (dropPAW_greaterThan_ITS_PCV), the ventilator should immediately trigger a new breathing cycle (initiate the inspiration phase). The translation uses the atomic propositions 'PCVMode' (system is in PCV mode), 'expiratoryPhase' (expiratory phase is active), 'dropPAW_greaterThan_ITS_PCV' (sudden pressure drop above inhale trigger sensitivity), and 'breathingCycleStart' (start of a new breath cycle). The use of the 'upon' and 'immediately satisfy' templates captures this causality and timing.
decision1_substring: Within the trigger window during the expiratory phase, in the case of spontaneous breathing
decision2_substring: when it detects a sudden drop in pressure above the inhale trigger sensitivity (i.e., it yields the condition drop(PAW) > ITS_PCV )

The atomic propositions available make it clear that 'expiratoryPhase' marks the expiratory phase, and 'dropPAW_greaterThan_ITS_PCV' encodes the key detection event triggering a breath. While there is no explicit atomic proposition for the 'trigger window during the expiratory phase,' the strongest possible mapping is to restrict the behavior to 'while expiratoryPhase,' and to map the triggering event to 'upon dropPAW_greaterThan_ITS_PCV,' requiring 'inspiratoryPhase' to follow (i.e., a new breathing cycle starts by going to inspiration phase). Thus, the structured NL follows: while expiratoryPhase, whenever dropPAW_greaterThan_ITS_PCV, at the next timepoint satisfy inspiratoryPhase.
